title: SQL Injection Error Signal Observed
description: Replay-based mutation produced a database-style error response consistent with SQL injection handling weaknesses.
impact: SQL injection can lead to data theft, authentication bypass, and server-side compromise depending on database privileges.
likelihood: High when crafted input consistently triggers database error behavior on an exposed route.
recommendations: Use parameterized queries and prepared statements for all dynamic database access.
Validate and constrain server-side input handling at every trust boundary.
Review database error handling to avoid disclosing backend details to clients.
